title: Coriandrum latifolium
taxonomy: Coriandrum latifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Coriandrum, which includes a few other species of coriander. The plant's classification is also related to its botanical name, which is often used in scientific research and identification. Coriandrum latifolium is thought to be native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The specific classification of the plant can be further broken down into its rank, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.
uses: Coriandrum latifolium, also known as broad-leaved coriander, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits. It is said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. In addition, the plant is believed to have carminative properties, helping to relieve digestive issues such as bloating and gas. The leaves of Coriandrum latifolium are also used to treat fever and reduce inflammation. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of this plant.

side_effects: Coriandrum latifolium is not a commonly known species, so information on its side effects is scarce.
